_comment: Per-patch SHA-256 manifest (#104a). Records the expected hash of every patches/*.patch so silent drift is caught: preflight Gate P recomputes each patch's SHA-256 and FAILS if it differs from the value here. Editing a patch REQUIRES updating its hash in this file (review-gated), which is the whole point — a patch can't change unreviewed. This is DISTINCT from the aggregate `gjoa.build.engine-patch-hash` baked into the binary by tools/prep/fingerprint.bjs (a 12-char fold-hash computed at build time for provenance); this file is the per-patch, tracked, drift-gating record. Regenerate with: cd patches && for f in $(ls *.patch | sort); do echo "$f $(sha256sum "$f" | cut -d' ' -f1)"; done
